P8106_HW5_sp4436

Sukhman Parhar

2026-05-06

Question 1

Clean the data

# Load in the data 
auto <- read_csv("auto.csv") %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  as.data.frame()
## Rows: 392 Columns: 8
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (1): mpg_cat
## dbl (7): cylinders, displacement, horsepower, weight, acceleration, year, or...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
# Remove cars with 3 cylinders
auto <- auto[auto$cylinders != 3, ]

# Convert Outcome (mpg_cat) to factor
auto$mpg_cat <- factor(auto$mpg_cat)

# Convert Origin to factor 
auto$origin <- as.factor(auto$origin)

#summary(auto)

Split the data

set.seed(2)

split <- initial_split(auto, prop = 0.7)

train_data <- training(split)
test_data  <- testing(split)

# set up cross validation control
ctrl <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10,
                     summaryFunction = twoClassSummary,
                     classProbs = TRUE)

Part A: Fit a support vector classifier (SVC) to the training data. What are the training and

test error rates?

set.seed(2)

# Support vector classifier: linear kernel
linear.tune <- tune.svm(mpg_cat ~ .,
                        data = train_data,
                        kernel = "linear",
                        cost = exp(seq(-5, 2, len = 50)),
                        scale = TRUE)

plot(linear.tune, highlight = TRUE, xTrans = log)

best.linear <- linear.tune$best.model

pred.linear.train <- predict(best.linear, newdata = train_data)
pred.linear.test  <- predict(best.linear, newdata = test_data)

mean(pred.linear.train != train_data$mpg_cat)
## [1] 0.06642066
svc.test.error <- mean(pred.linear.test != test_data$mpg_cat)
svc.test.error
## [1] 0.08547009
Training and test error

Training error = 0.06642066 Test error = 0.08547009

Part B: Fit a support vector machine (SVM) with a radial kernel to the training data. What

are the training and test error rates?

Tuning Grid
svmr.grid = expand.grid(C = exp(seq(1, 7, len = 50)),
                        sigma = exp(seq(-10, -2, len = 20)))
Tune
set.seed(2)

svmr.fit = train(mpg_cat ~ . ,
                 data = train_data,
                 method = "svmRadialSigma",
                 tuneGrid = svmr.grid,
                 trControl = ctrl)
## Warning in train.default(x, y, weights = w, ...): The metric "Accuracy" was not
## in the result set. ROC will be used instead.
myCol = rainbow(25)
myPar = list(superpose.symbol = list(myCol),
             superpose.line = list(col = myCol))

plot(svmr.fit, highlight = TRUE, par.settings = myPar)

svmr.fit$bestTune
##        sigma        C
## 476 0.025117 45.43699
confusionMatrix(svmr.fit)
## Cross-Validated (10 fold) Confusion Matrix 
## 
## (entries are percentual average cell counts across resamples)
##  
##           Reference
## Prediction high  low
##       high 48.0  5.2
##       low   2.6 44.3
##                             
##  Accuracy (average) : 0.9225
pred.train.rad <- predict(svmr.fit, newdata = train_data)
pred.test.rad  <- predict(svmr.fit, newdata = test_data)

train.error.rad <- mean(pred.train.rad != train_data$mpg_cat)
test.error.rad  <- mean(pred.test.rad  != test_data$mpg_cat)

train.error.rad
## [1] 0.05535055
test.error.rad
## [1] 0.05982906
Training and test error

Training error = 0.05535055 Test error = 0.05982906

Part C: Compare the performance of the above models with boosting. Do SVC or SVM

provide better predictive performance on this dataset?

gbmA.grid = expand.grid(n.trees = c(100, 200, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000, 10000),
                        interaction.depth = 1:5,
                        shrinkage = c(0.005, 0.007, 0.009),
                        n.minobsinnode = 10)

set.seed(2)

gbmA.fit = train(mpg_cat ~ . ,
                 train_data,
                 tuneGrid = gbmA.grid,
                 trControl = ctrl,
                 method = "gbm",
                 distribution = "adaboost",
                 metric = "Accuracy",
                 verbose = FALSE)
## Warning in train.default(x, y, weights = w, ...): The metric "Accuracy" was not
## in the result set. ROC will be used instead.
ggplot(gbmA.fit, highlight = TRUE)
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the caret package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/topepo/caret/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

pred.gbm.train <- predict(gbmA.fit, newdata = train_data)
pred.gbm.test  <- predict(gbmA.fit, newdata = test_data)

train.error.gbm <- mean(pred.gbm.train != train_data$mpg_cat)
test.error.gbm  <- mean(pred.gbm.test  != test_data$mpg_cat)

train.error.gbm
## [1] 0.02583026
test.error.gbm
## [1] 0.05982906
svc.test.error
## [1] 0.08547009
test.error.rad
## [1] 0.05982906
test.error.gbm
## [1] 0.05982906
